name: Ask User Questions MCP Server
what_it_does: Ask User Questions MCP server enabling AI agents to pause and ask users clarifying questions during task execution — presenting multiple-choice or free-text questions to users, collecting structured responses, handling optional default answers, and integrating user input collection into agent-driven interactive workflows where agent certainty is insufficient for autonomous proceeding.
use_cases: Collecting user preferences before executing ambiguous tasks from interactive agents
Asking clarifying questions when task requirements are underspecified from helpful agents
Presenting decision options to users before taking irreversible actions from safety agents
Gathering additional context mid-task when new questions arise from adaptive agents
Building interactive agent tutorials and onboarding flows from educational agents
Implementing structured feedback collection during complex multi-step workflows from orchestration agents
not_for: Fully automated pipelines where user interaction is not available or desired
High-throughput batch processing without human availability
Environments where terminal/CLI interaction is not supported
best_when: An agent needs to interactively collect input from the user during task execution — complementary to human-in-the-loop approval patterns but focused on information gathering rather than approval.
avoid_when: Your pipeline is fully automated or you need asynchronous input collection — this requires synchronous user response.

auth.notes: No authentication — interactive CLI tool. Requires terminal/console access for user to respond.

pricing.notes: Free, open source community MCP for interactive user questioning.

performance.rate_limits: Human-bound — latency depends on user response time

known_agent_gotchas: Agent blocks until user responds — implement timeouts with default answers for unattended runs
User must be at terminal — not suitable for headless server or batch processing
Question format (multiple choice vs free text) should be chosen based on expected user expertise
No validation of user responses by default — implement validation in agent if needed
Community MCP — minimal documentation for advanced interactive patterns
Excessive questioning degrades UX — use judiciously rather than asking for every decision
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local interactive tool. No external calls. No credentials. User responses are captured locally. Community MCP. Safe for general use.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local interactive. No external calls. No credentials. Safe community MCP.
